entries.problem: api/discord.js appelle .json() sur une réponse SSE (text/event-stream) de Yahoo Finance
entries.commit: fix(discord): guard against SSE/non-JSON response from Yahoo Finance in getQuickSignal
entries.learned: Yahoo Finance peut retourner text/event-stream ou d'autres content-types non-JSON, il faut toujours wrapper .json() dans un try/catch ET vérifier le content-type avant d'appeler .json().

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: api/brain.js uses VERCEL_URL env var which is empty in production deployments
entries.commit: fix: use APP_URL instead of unreliable VERCEL_URL in brain.js
entries.learned: VERCEL_URL is a build-time variable that can point to preview deployments, making it unreliable for server-side self-referential fetch calls — APP_URL should always be the canonical production URL source.

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: api/trading-expert.js fetch() calls without AbortSignal.timeout() — can hang indefinitely
entries.commit: fix: guard against SSE/non-JSON responses in trading-expert fetchStockData
entries.learned: The Yahoo Finance SSE content-type bug (already fixed in discord.js and morning.js) was not yet applied to trading-expert.js's fetchStockData(), leaving it vulnerable to the same crash when Yahoo returns text/event-stream instead of JSON.

entries.problem: fetch() calls in api/andy.js have no AbortSignal.timeout() — can hang indefinitely on Yahoo Finance and CoinGecko requests
entries.commit: fix: add AbortSignal.timeout() to all fetch calls in api/andy.js to prevent indefinite hangs
entries.learned: All external fetch() calls in Vercel serverless functions must have explicit timeouts since the platform's execution limit is hard and a stalled TCP connection will silently exhaust it.

findings.description: Calling .json() on an SSE (text/event-stream) response — must read stream instead

entries.note: Patterns detected before Claude analysis — guiding fix priority

entries.problem: api/morning.js githubGet() calls .json() directly without content-type check, and the JSON.parse on base64 content will throw uncaught if the file is invalid — silent crash in loadMemorySummary/loadAgentsRegistry
entries.commit: fix: add AbortSignal.timeout, content-type check and safe JSON parse to githubGet in morning.js
entries.learned: githubGet was the only fetch helper in the codebase without a timeout, and its double-parse path (r.json() then JSON.parse(base64)) lacked individual error boundaries — a rate-limit HTML response from GitHub would silently propagate null through the entire morning briefing pipeline.
